name: Conflict Resolver
description: Detects and resolves conflicts following the three-step protocol (C009). Detection: two or more nodes produce different values for the same field within the same domain during the same circuit execution, identified by correlating OTLP spans by execution ID with buffering/windowing (rabbit hole patch — configurable window timeout). Step 1: authority check — authoritative node wins if trust > authority_override_floor (0.4), else human review. Step 2: trust arbitration — higher-trust node wins if trust delta > threshold (0.2). Step 3: unresolvable — flag to human, emit to Stigmergy as high-weight signal, block deploy if protected tier. Maintains append-only conflict records with resolution status in JSONL format with SHA256 checksums. Provides unresolved conflict list for CLI and report. Supports N-way conflicts (not just pairwise). Trust scores are snapshotted at detection time for deterministic resolution.

types.name: ResolutionStatus
types.description: Lifecycle status of a conflict record. Transitions: DETECTED -> (AUTHORITY_RESOLVED | TRUST_RESOLVED | UNRESOLVABLE) -> HUMAN_REVIEWED (only from UNRESOLVABLE).

types.name: ResolutionStrategy
types.description: Which step of the three-step protocol resolved the conflict.

types.name: ConflictErrorCode
types.description: Machine-readable error codes for all conflict resolver error responses. Included in all error payloads per operating procedures.

types.name: NodeValue

fields.name: node_id
fields.description: Unique identifier of the reporting node.

fields.name: value_serialized
fields.description: JSON-serialized value reported by this node. Serialized at ingestion for deterministic comparison.

fields.name: trust_score_snapshot
fields.description: Trust score of the node at conflict detection time, snapshotted from trust ledger.

fields.name: is_authoritative
fields.description: Whether this node is declared authoritative for the domain+field per the authority registry.

fields.name: span_id
fields.description: OTLP span ID from which this value was extracted, for traceability.

types.description: A single node's reported value for a contested field, with trust score snapshotted at detection time. Value is serialized as a JSON-compatible string to ensure deterministic comparison and storage.

types.name: Resolution

fields.name: strategy
fields.description: Which step of the three-step protocol produced this resolution.

fields.name: winner_node_id
fields.description: Node ID of the winning node. Empty string if strategy is HUMAN and not yet decided.

fields.name: resolved_at
fields.description: UTC ISO-8601 timestamp when resolution was determined. Must be UTC per operating procedures.

fields.name: rationale
fields.description: Human-readable explanation of why this resolution was chosen, including specific node IDs, trust scores, and thresholds.

fields.name: reviewed_by
fields.description: Identity of human reviewer if strategy is HUMAN. Empty string if not human-reviewed.

types.description: The outcome of conflict resolution: which strategy was used, who won, and rationale.

types.name: ConflictRecord

fields.name: conflict_id
fields.description: Globally unique conflict identifier (UUID4 string).

validators.error_message: conflict_id must be a valid UUID4

fields.name: execution_id
fields.description: Circuit execution ID that produced the conflicting values.

fields.name: domain
fields.description: Domain in which the conflict occurred.

fields.name: field
fields.description: Field name that has conflicting values.

fields.name: data_tier
fields.description: Classification tier of the field from the field classifier, used for deploy-blocking decisions. Empty string if tier lookup failed (degraded mode).

fields.name: competing_values
fields.description: All competing node values. Must contain at least 2 entries (a conflict requires disagreement).

validators.error_message: A conflict requires at least 2 competing values

fields.name: detected_at
fields.description: UTC ISO-8601 timestamp when the conflict was detected.

fields.name: status
fields.description: Current lifecycle status of this conflict.

fields.name: resolution
fields.description: Resolution details. Only present when status is not DETECTED.

fields.name: blocks_deploy
fields.description: True if this conflict blocks deployment. Set based on data_tier being a protected tier and status being UNRESOLVABLE or DETECTED.

types.description: A single conflict instance: N nodes producing different values for the same (execution_id, domain, field) tuple. Append-only — once written to the conflict log, records are never modified in place; resolution is appended as a new log entry referencing the conflict_id.

types.name: ConflictResolverConfig

fields.name: window_timeout_seconds
fields.description: How long to buffer spans for a given (execution_id, domain, field) window before closing it and checking for conflicts. Lazy timeout — checked on next ingest or explicit flush.

fields.name: authority_override_floor
fields.description: Minimum trust score an authoritative node must have to win via authority check (Step 1). Below this, conflict goes to human review.

fields.name: trust_delta_threshold
fields.description: Minimum trust score difference between the highest and second-highest trust nodes for trust arbitration to win (Step 2).

fields.name: checkpoint_interval
fields.description: Number of conflict log entries between SHA256 checksum lines in the JSONL conflict log. Matches trust ledger pattern.

fields.name: conflict_log_path
fields.description: Path to the append-only JSONL conflict log file. Must use pathlib internally.

fields.name: protected_tiers
fields.description: List of data classification tier names that block deploy when unresolved conflicts exist.

types.description: Configuration for the conflict resolver. Loaded from YAML config per operating procedures. All thresholds validated to [0.0, 1.0].

types.name: ConflictSignal

fields.name: signal_type
fields.description: Always 'conflict_unresolvable'.

fields.name: conflict_id
fields.description: References the ConflictRecord.conflict_id.

fields.name: execution_id
fields.description: Circuit execution ID.

fields.name: domain
fields.description: Domain of the conflict.

fields.name: field
fields.description: Contested field name.

fields.name: competing_node_ids
fields.description: Node IDs of all competing nodes.

fields.name: max_trust_score
fields.description: Highest trust score among competing nodes.

fields.name: trust_delta
fields.description: Difference between highest and second-highest trust scores.

fields.name: weight
fields.description: Signal weight. Always 1.0 for unresolvable conflicts (high-weight).

validators.error_message: weight must be 1.0 for unresolvable conflicts

fields.name: emitted_at
fields.description: UTC ISO-8601 timestamp.

fields.name: blocks_deploy
fields.description: Whether this conflict blocks deployment.

types.description: Signal emitted to Stigmergy for unresolvable conflicts. High-weight signal per C009 spec.

types.name: SpanFieldReport

fields.name: span_id
fields.description: OTLP span ID.

fields.name: execution_id
fields.description: Circuit execution ID extracted from span attributes.

fields.name: node_id
fields.description: Node that produced this span.

fields.name: domain
fields.description: Domain of the reported field.

fields.name: field
fields.description: Field name.

fields.name: value_serialized
fields.description: JSON-serialized field value reported by the node.

fields.name: reported_at
fields.description: UTC ISO-8601 timestamp from the span.

types.description: Extracted field report from an OTLP span. This is the input to the conflict detector. Produced by the OTLP subscriber layer.

types.name: ConflictSummary

fields.name: total_conflicts
fields.description: Total number of conflict records.

fields.name: unresolved_count
fields.description: Number of conflicts with status DETECTED or UNRESOLVABLE.

fields.name: authority_resolved_count
fields.description: Conflicts resolved by authority check.

fields.name: trust_resolved_count
fields.description: Conflicts resolved by trust arbitration.

fields.name: human_reviewed_count
fields.description: Conflicts resolved by human review.

fields.name: deploy_blocking_count
fields.description: Number of conflicts currently blocking deployment.

fields.name: domains_affected
fields.description: Distinct domains with unresolved conflicts.

types.description: Summary statistics for CLI and reporting.

types.name: TrustLookup

fields.name: lookup_trust_score
fields.description: Callable protocol: (node_id: str) -> float. Returns raw trust score from ledger.

types.description: Protocol type for trust score lookup dependency. Implemented via composition, not inheritance.

types.name: AuthorityLookup

fields.name: is_authoritative
fields.description: Callable protocol: (node_id: str, domain: str, field: str) -> bool. Returns whether node is declared authoritative.

types.description: Protocol type for authority registry dependency. Implemented via composition, not inheritance.

types.name: SignalEmitter

fields.name: emit_signal
fields.description: Callable protocol: (signal: ConflictSignal) -> None. Emits to Stigmergy.

types.description: Protocol type for stigmergy signal emission dependency. Implemented via composition, not inheritance.

types.name: ConflictStore

fields.name: append
fields.description: Callable protocol: (record: ConflictRecord) -> None. Appends record to JSONL log.

fields.name: load_all
fields.description: Callable protocol: () -> list[ConflictRecord]. Loads and verifies all records from log.

fields.name: verify_checksums
fields.description: Callable protocol: () -> bool. Verifies all SHA256 checksum lines in the log.

types.description: Protocol type for conflict persistence. Append-only JSONL store with SHA256 checksums at checkpoint_interval boundaries. Matches trust ledger pattern.

functions.name: ingest
functions.description: Ingests a span field report into the conflict detection buffer. Buffers by (execution_id, domain, field) key. When a window closes (lazy timeout checked on each ingest call), emits any detected conflicts where 2+ distinct values exist for the same key. Deduplicates by (node_id, span_id) to handle retransmitted spans. If only one distinct value exists after window close, no conflict is emitted.

inputs.name: span_report
inputs.description: Extracted field report from an OTLP span.

error_cases.name: trust_lookup_failed
error_cases.condition: Trust ledger lookup fails for a node during conflict detection snapshot

error_data.node_id: the node whose trust could not be looked up

error_cases.name: authority_lookup_failed
error_cases.condition: Authority registry lookup fails for a node during conflict detection snapshot

error_data.node_id: the node whose authority could not be looked up

error_cases.name: store_write_failed
error_cases.condition: Conflict store append fails during persistence

error_data.conflict_id: the conflict that failed to persist

error_cases.name: duplicate_span
error_cases.condition: Span with same node_id + span_id already in buffer (not an error, silently ignored)

error_cases.name: tier_lookup_failed
error_cases.condition: Field classifier tier lookup fails; conflict is still detected with empty data_tier (degraded mode)

preconditions: ConflictDetector has been initialized with valid ConflictResolverConfig
TrustLookup and AuthorityLookup dependencies are available for snapshot at detection time
postconditions: Returned list contains only newly detected conflicts (status=DETECTED) from windows that closed during this call
Each returned ConflictRecord has competing_values.length >= 2
Each NodeValue.trust_score_snapshot reflects the trust score at detection time, not ingestion time
Each NodeValue.is_authoritative reflects current authority registry state
All returned ConflictRecords have been persisted to the conflict store
span_report is buffered if its window has not yet closed
Duplicate spans (same node_id + span_id) are silently dropped
side_effects: Persists detected ConflictRecords to JSONL conflict log
Snapshots trust scores from trust ledger

structured_side_effects.target: in-memory span buffer keyed by (execution_id, domain, field)
structured_side_effects.description: Adds span to buffer or closes window and clears buffer entry
Appends newly detected ConflictRecord entries
Snapshots trust scores for all nodes in a closing window
Checks authority status for all nodes in a closing window
Looks up data tier for the contested field
Logs window open, close, and conflict detection events

performance_budget.complexity: O(W) where W is the number of windows checked for timeout on this call

functions.name: flush
functions.description: Force-closes all open detection windows regardless of timeout, emitting any detected conflicts. Used during shutdown, circuit completion, or manual CLI trigger. After flush, the buffer is empty.

error_cases.name: trust_lookup_failed
error_cases.condition: Trust ledger lookup fails for a node during flush

error_cases.name: authority_lookup_failed
error_cases.condition: Authority registry lookup fails during flush

error_cases.name: store_write_failed
error_cases.condition: Conflict store append fails during flush persistence

preconditions: ConflictDetector has been initialized with valid ConflictResolverConfig
postconditions: All open windows are closed and evaluated for conflicts
Returned list contains all newly detected conflicts from flushed windows
In-memory buffer is empty after flush completes
All returned ConflictRecords have been persisted to the conflict store
Windows with only one distinct value produce no conflict
side_effects: Persists detected ConflictRecords to JSONL conflict log

structured_side_effects.description: Clears all buffer entries after evaluation
Appends newly detected ConflictRecord entries
Snapshots trust scores for nodes in all open windows
Checks authority for nodes in all open windows
Looks up data tiers for all contested fields
Logs flush initiation, window count, and conflicts detected

performance_budget.complexity: O(B) where B is total buffered spans across all windows

functions.name: resolve
functions.description: Runs the three-step conflict resolution protocol on a detected conflict. Step 1: Authority check — if exactly one authoritative node exists and its trust > authority_override_floor, it wins (AUTHORITY_RESOLVED). If authoritative node trust <= floor, skip to Step 3. Step 2: Trust arbitration — sort competing nodes by trust_score_snapshot descending; if delta between #1 and #2 > trust_delta_threshold, #1 wins (TRUST_RESOLVED). For N-way conflicts, only the top-2 trust scores matter for the delta check. Step 3: Unresolvable — set status to UNRESOLVABLE, emit ConflictSignal to stigmergy, set blocks_deploy if data_tier is protected. Returns the updated ConflictRecord. The original DETECTED record and the resolved record are both in the append-only log.

inputs.name: conflict
inputs.description: The conflict to resolve. Must have status DETECTED.

error_cases.name: already_resolved
error_cases.condition: conflict.status is not DETECTED

error_cases.name: signal_emission_failed
error_cases.condition: Stigmergy signal emission fails during Step 3 (unresolvable)

error_cases.name: store_write_failed
error_cases.condition: Conflict store append fails when persisting resolution

error_cases.name: empty_competing_values
error_cases.condition: conflict.competing_values has fewer than 2 entries (should not happen if preconditions hold)

preconditions: All NodeValue.trust_score_snapshot values are already populated (snapshotted at detection)
ConflictResolver has been initialized with valid config and all Protocol dependencies
postconditions: Returned ConflictRecord.status is one of: AUTHORITY_RESOLVED, TRUST_RESOLVED, UNRESOLVABLE
Returned ConflictRecord.resolution is populated with strategy, winner_node_id, resolved_at (UTC), and rationale
If status is AUTHORITY_RESOLVED: resolution.strategy == AUTHORITY and winner is the authoritative node
If status is TRUST_RESOLVED: resolution.strategy == TRUST_ARBITRATION and winner is the highest-trust node
If status is UNRESOLVABLE: resolution.strategy == HUMAN and a ConflictSignal has been emitted to stigmergy
If status is UNRESOLVABLE and data_tier in protected_tiers: blocks_deploy == True
Resolution is deterministic given the same conflict record and config
Updated record has been appended to the conflict store (append-only log)
rationale includes specific node IDs, trust scores, and threshold values used in the decision
side_effects: Persists resolved ConflictRecord to JSONL log
Emits ConflictSignal to stigmergy if unresolvable

structured_side_effects.description: Appends the resolved ConflictRecord entry
Emits ConflictSignal if conflict is unresolvable (Step 3)
Logs each step attempted and the final resolution outcome with rationale

performance_budget.complexity: O(N log N) where N is number of competing values (sort by trust score)

functions.name: submit_human_review
functions.description: Records a human review decision for an UNRESOLVABLE conflict. Transitions the conflict from UNRESOLVABLE to HUMAN_REVIEWED with the selected winner node and reviewer identity. Only valid for conflicts with status UNRESOLVABLE.

inputs.name: conflict_id
inputs.description: UUID of the conflict to review.

inputs.name: winner_node_id
inputs.description: Node ID selected as the winner by the human reviewer. Must be one of the competing node IDs.

error_cases.name: conflict_not_found
error_cases.condition: No conflict with the given conflict_id exists

error_cases.name: invalid_status
error_cases.condition: Conflict status is not UNRESOLVABLE

error_cases.name: invalid_winner
error_cases.condition: winner_node_id is not among the competing node IDs

error_cases.name: store_write_failed
error_cases.condition: Conflict store append fails

preconditions: Conflict with conflict_id exists in the store
winner_node_id is one of the competing node IDs in the conflict
postconditions: Returned ConflictRecord.status == HUMAN_REVIEWED
Returned ConflictRecord.resolution.strategy == HUMAN
Returned ConflictRecord.resolution.winner_node_id == winner_node_id
Returned ConflictRecord.resolution.reviewed_by == reviewed_by
Returned ConflictRecord.blocks_deploy == False (human review clears deploy block)
Updated record has been appended to the conflict store
If previous record had blocks_deploy == True, the deploy block is now cleared
side_effects: Persists human-reviewed ConflictRecord to JSONL log

structured_side_effects.description: Appends the human-reviewed ConflictRecord entry
Updates the latest status for this conflict_id
Logs human review submission with reviewer identity and conflict_id

functions.name: get_unresolved
functions.description: Returns all conflicts that are not yet fully resolved (status DETECTED or UNRESOLVABLE), optionally filtered by domain. Used by CLI and report generation. Returns newest-first ordering.

inputs.name: domain
inputs.description: Optional domain filter. If empty string, returns unresolved conflicts across all domains.

error_cases.name: store_read_failed
error_cases.condition: Conflict store read fails

postconditions: All returned records have status DETECTED or UNRESOLVABLE
If domain is non-empty, all returned records have matching domain
Results are ordered by detected_at descending (newest first)
Result is a snapshot — new conflicts detected after this call are not included

structured_side_effects.description: Reads conflict records to filter unresolved entries

performance_budget.complexity: O(C) where C is total conflict records

functions.name: has_blocking_conflicts
functions.description: Checks whether any unresolved conflicts in the given domain block deployment. A conflict blocks deploy if blocks_deploy == True (determined by data_tier being in protected_tiers and status being DETECTED or UNRESOLVABLE). Used as a deploy gate.

inputs.name: domain
inputs.description: Domain to check for deploy-blocking conflicts.

error_cases.name: store_read_failed
error_cases.condition: Conflict store read fails

postconditions: Returns True if and only if at least one conflict exists with domain == domain AND blocks_deploy == True AND status in (DETECTED, UNRESOLVABLE)
Returns False if no such conflict exists or domain has no conflicts

structured_side_effects.description: Reads conflict records to check for deploy-blocking entries

performance_budget.complexity: O(C_d) where C_d is conflict records in the given domain

functions.name: get_summary
functions.description: Returns aggregate conflict statistics for CLI display and reporting. Provides counts by resolution status, deploy-blocking count, and list of affected domains.

error_cases.name: store_read_failed
error_cases.condition: Conflict store read fails

postconditions: total_conflicts == sum of all status counts
unresolved_count == count of DETECTED + UNRESOLVABLE
deploy_blocking_count == count of records where blocks_deploy == True AND status in (DETECTED, UNRESOLVABLE)
domains_affected contains only domains with unresolved conflicts, deduplicated and sorted

structured_side_effects.description: Reads all conflict records to compute summary statistics

performance_budget.complexity: O(C) where C is total conflict records

functions.name: verify_log_integrity
functions.description: Verifies the SHA256 checksums in the append-only JSONL conflict log. Returns True if all checksum lines are valid, False otherwise. Used for audit and startup integrity checks.

error_cases.name: store_read_failed
error_cases.condition: Conflict log file cannot be read

error_cases.name: checksum_mismatch
error_cases.condition: A checksum line does not match the computed hash (returns False, does not raise)

preconditions: Conflict log file exists at conflict_log_path
postconditions: Returns True if and only if every SHA256 checksum line in the log matches the hash of the preceding N entries (where N is checkpoint_interval)
Returns True if log is empty (vacuously true)
Does not modify the log file

structured_side_effects.description: Reads entire conflict log to verify all SHA256 checksum lines
Logs verification start, any mismatches found, and final result

performance_budget.complexity: O(L) where L is total lines in the conflict log

functions.name: load_config
functions.description: Loads and validates ConflictResolverConfig from a YAML file. All threshold validators are applied. Returns a validated config instance.

inputs.name: config_path
inputs.description: Path to the YAML configuration file. Resolved via pathlib.

error_cases.name: file_not_found
error_cases.condition: Config file does not exist at config_path

error_cases.name: invalid_yaml
error_cases.condition: File content is not valid YAML

error_cases.name: invalid_config
error_cases.condition: YAML content fails Pydantic validation for ConflictResolverConfig

preconditions: File at config_path exists and is readable
postconditions: Returned config passes all ConflictResolverConfig validators
All threshold values are within [0.0, 1.0]
checkpoint_interval is >= 1
window_timeout_seconds is >= 0.1

structured_side_effects.description: Reads YAML configuration file from disk via pathlib

trust_ledger: Trust score computation is deterministic given the same ledger input
authority_registry: is_authoritative(node_id: str, domain: str, field: str) -> bool
Authority is declared from manifests, not computed
field_classifier: Returns classification tier for deploy-blocking decisions

invariants: The conflict log is append-only: records are never modified or deleted in place. Resolution is appended as a new log entry referencing the original conflict_id.
Trust scores in NodeValue.trust_score_snapshot are immutable after detection — they reflect the trust state at detection time, not current trust.
Resolution is deterministic: given the same ConflictRecord (with snapshotted trust scores and authority flags) and the same ConflictResolverConfig, resolve() always produces the same result.
A conflict requires at least 2 competing values with distinct value_serialized — a single value or all-identical values never produce a conflict.
Status transitions are strictly ordered: DETECTED -> (AUTHORITY_RESOLVED | TRUST_RESOLVED | UNRESOLVABLE) -> HUMAN_REVIEWED. No other transitions are valid.
HUMAN_REVIEWED is only reachable from UNRESOLVABLE. AUTHORITY_RESOLVED and TRUST_RESOLVED are terminal states.
blocks_deploy is True only when data_tier is in protected_tiers AND status is DETECTED or UNRESOLVABLE. Human review clears the deploy block.
SHA256 checksum lines appear in the conflict log every checkpoint_interval entries, matching the trust ledger checksum pattern.
All timestamps are UTC ISO-8601 strings. No local time is ever stored or compared.

Trust is earned (from ledger), authority is declared (from registry) — the resolver never conflates them. Authority check uses is_authoritative AND trust_score_snapshot; trust arbitration uses only trust_score_snapshot.
Error messages always include the specific conflict_id, node_id, domain, or field that caused the error, per operating procedures.
For N-way conflicts (>2 nodes), Step 1 authority check requires exactly one authoritative node among competitors. Step 2 trust arbitration compares the top-2 trust scores regardless of N.
